formulas.name: Kredong Apostolico
formulas.default_text:
**Sumasampalataya ako sa Diyos,
        ang Amang Makapangyarihan-sa-lahat,
        maylikha ng langit at lupa.
Sumasampalataya ako kay Jesu-Cristo,
        ang Kanyang bugtong na Anak, na ating Panginoon.
        Siya ay ipinagdalantao sa pamamagitan ng Banal na Espiritu,
        at ipinanganak ni Birheng Maria.
        Nagdusa sa ilalim ni Poncio Pilato,
        ipinako sa krus, namatay, at inilibing.
        Siya ay bumaba sa mga patay.
        Sa ikatlong araw Siya ay nabuhay na muli;
        Siya ay umakyat sa langit,
        Siya ay naluklok sa kanang kamay ng Ama,
        at mula roon Siya ay babalik upang hatulan
        ang mga buhay at ang mga patay.
Sumasampalataya ako sa Banal na Espiritu,
        sa banal na pangkalahatang Iglesya,
        sa kapulungan ng mga banal,
        sa kapatawaran ng mga kasalanan,
        sa muling pagkabuhay ng katawan,
        at sa buhay na walang hanggan. Amen.**

formulas.name: Apostles’ Creed
formulas.default_text:
**I believe in God, the Father almighty,
        maker of heaven and earth.
I believe in Jesus Christ, his only Son, our Lord.
        He was conceived by the Holy Spirit
        and born of the Virgin Mary.
        He suffered under Pontius Pilate,
        was crucified, died, and was buried;
        He descended to the dead.
        On the third day he rose again;
        He ascended into heaven,
        He is seated at the right hand of the Father.
        and He will come again
        to judge the living and the dead.
        to judge the living and the dead.
I believe in the Holy Spirit,
        the holy catholic Church,
        the communion of saints,
        the forgiveness of sins,
        the resurrection of the body,
        and the life everlasting. Amen.**

formulas.name: Trinitarian Seal (FIL)
formulas.default_text: Sa pangalan ng Ama, ng Anak, at ng Banal na Espiritu. Amen.

formulas.name: Absolution
formulas.default_text:
Sumasampalataya ba kayo sa ebanghelyong ito?
Sumasampalataya kami.
Kung gayon, sa pangalan ni Cristo, at sa awtoridad ng Kaniyang Salita, habang kayo’y nagpapatotoo sa inyong sariling paniniwala sa ebanghelyo, ipinahahayag ng Kasulatan sa inyo na ang inyong mga kasalanan ay pinatawad na, at kayo’y wala na sa ilalim ng kaparusahan ng Diyos. Sa pangalan ng Ama, ng Anak, at ng Banal na Espiritu.
Amen.

formulas.name: Trinitarian Seal (ENG)
formulas.default_text: In the name of the Father, of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.

prayers.text:
**Makapangyarihang Diyos,
na ang kaluwalhatian ay pumupuno sa mga langit, at ang Iyong trono ay nakatayo sa itaas
ng mga Kerubin,
ipinahahayag namin na kami ay nagkasala
laban sa Iyo sa aming mga iniisip,
mga salita, at mga gawa.
Dinungisan namin ang Iyong Banal na Pangalan at tinalikuran ang Iyong kautusan.
Hindi namin hinanap  ang Iyong mukha
nang buong puso,
at marami kaming mabubuting gawa
na hindi nagawa.
Sindihan Mo sa amin ang banal na dalamhati para sa aming paghihimagsik
at para sa katuwirang aming pinabayaan.
O Panginoon, Ikaw ay matuwid sa paghatol, ngunit mayaman sa awa at tapat sa pag-ibig. Sa Iyo ay may masaganang kapatawaran.
Pagalingin Mo ang aming pagkawasak, liwanagan Mo ang aming madilim na isipan, patatagin Mo ang aming mahihinang kalooban, at pagkalooban Mo ng kapayapaan ang aming mga balisang kaluluwa. Amen.**

**Almighty God, whose glory fills the heavens,
and whose throne stands above the Cherubim,
we confess that we have sinned against You
in our thoughts, words, and deeds.
We have defiled Your Holy Name
and forsaken Your law.
We have not sought Your face with all of our hearts,
and we have left many good works undone.
Kindle in us a godly sorrow for our rebellion
and the righteousness we have neglected.
O Lord, You are righteous in judgment,
yet rich in mercy and steadfast love.
With You is abundant forgiveness.
Heal our brokenness,
enlighten our darkened minds,
fortify our weak wills,
and grant peace to our restless souls. Amen.**

1. ADORATION — Explicit acknowledgement of God’s supreme greatness and goodness.
2. HUMBLE APPROACH — Recognition of God’s mercy and a humble acknowledgment of our own unrighteousness as we approach His throne.
3. ACCEPTANCE — A petition for the Holy Spirit’s strength to worship the Father, Son, and Spirit rightly, and that our Father would graciously accept our weak and lowly worship.
4. THANKSGIVING — Thanks to God for summoning us, allowing us to witness and experience His salvation once again through corporate worship.
5. TRINITARIAN CONCLUSION — May close with a fitting doxology, e.g. “...ito ang aming panalangin sa pangalan ng Panginoong Jesus, na nabubuhay at naghahari kasama Mo at ng Banal na Espiritu, iisa na Diyos, magpakailanman. Amen.” or “...sa pamamagitan ni Jesu-Cristo; sa Kanya nawa ang kaluwalhatian at kapangyarihan, kasama Mo at ng Banal na Espiritu, magpakailanman. Amen.”
Prepare in advance — write it out verbatim or meditate on these elements so the prayer is marked by reverence, clarity, and theological precision.

1. PETITION FOR UNDERSTANDING — A plea that the Holy Spirit would lift the veil of darkness from our hearts, illuminate our minds, and grant true spiritual understanding of the text.
2. HUMBLE SUBMISSION — An expression of submission to the authority, binding nature, and sufficiency of Holy Scripture.
3. FOCUS ON CHRIST — A prayer that Christ would be clearly revealed through the preaching, and that our eyes would be fixed on His work.
Trinitarian conclusion is optional, at the Minister’s discretion.

1. LAMENT — A holy complaint (Psalm 64:1) over the reality of mankind’s fallenness, the pain and misery it causes, and the divine judgement it ensures.
2. CONFESSION — Recognition of the corruption of our own hearts and the deeds of our flesh that rob us of joy in our salvation and zeal for God’s kingdom.
3. SUPPLICATION — A cry to God for His gracious pardon, for the mortification of our sins, for love for the appointed means of grace, and for the Holy Spirit’s work in our sanctification.
4. ASSURANCE — A Godward reminder of the precious promises of the gospel, claiming God’s own words to forgive His people and grant their prayers in the name of His Son.
5. TRINITARIAN CONCLUSION — May close with a fitting doxology, e.g. “...ito ang aming panalangin sa pangalan ng Panginoong Jesus, na nabubuhay at naghahari kasama Mo at ng Banal na Espiritu, iisa na Diyos, magpakailanman. Amen.” or “...sa pamamagitan ni Jesu-Cristo; sa Kanya nawa ang kaluwalhatian at kapangyarihan, kasama Mo at ng Banal na Espiritu, magpakailanman. Amen.”
Prepare in advance — write it out verbatim or meditate on these elements so the prayer is marked by reverence, clarity, and theological precision.

1. RENUNCIATION — An explicit stripping away of personal merit as we approach the table; contrast the immense holiness of the divine presence with our baseline unworthiness as fallen creatures.
2. APPEAL — Ground our bold approach to the Sacrament entirely in God’s unshakeable nature — His permanent character, which is always to extend grace to the brokenhearted.
3. PETITION — Emphasize that feeding upon Christ is an inward work executed strictly by faith, avoiding any carnal or physical misunderstanding; request the dual cleansing of our physical existence and our inner souls via the broken body and shed blood of Christ.
4. MYSTICAL UNION — A prayer for the visible seal and internal confirmation of the mystical union between Christ and the believer — an intimate reinforcement of our mutual indwelling, us in Christ and Christ in us.
5. CONFIRMATION — A petition that the Holy Spirit would use the physical elements to confirm, seal, and deepen gospel promises in the hearts of the Communicants.
Trinitarian conclusion is optional, at the Minister’s discretion.

1. THANKSGIVING — Deep gratitude for the rich mercy of God bestowed upon the congregation through the sacrament, and for the spiritual nourishment received through this means of grace.
2. CONFESSION — A humble plea for forgiveness regarding the structural flaws, straying thoughts, and spiritual defects present in the execution of the day’s worship.
3. GUIDANCE OF THE SPIRIT — A request for the gracious assistance and power of the Holy Spirit to attend the lives of the Communicants, that they would walk in the strength of the grace just received.
4. HOPEFUL EXPECTATION — An anchoring petition for strength to persevere in faith, hoping for the return of our Lord Jesus Christ from heaven, when we shall feast with Him at the final Marriage Supper of the Lamb.

1. THANKSGIVING FOR WORSHIP — Expressing gratitude for the morning service and the privilege of gathering.
2. CONSECRATION OF TITHES — Giving thanks for the offerings received and praying for their wise stewardship.
3. PRAYER OF APPLICATION — Thanking God for the sermon and praying for the long-term fruit of the Word in the lives of the people.
4. INTERCESSIONS & PETITIONS — General pastoral intercessions for the spiritual and physical needs of the congregation, the sister churches, and the global body of Christ; protection and provision for gospel ministers around the country; intercessions for the city and country, and for those in authority over them.

Hindi kami nangangahas na lumapit sa banal Mong hapag,
O mahabaging Panginoon,
na nagtitiwala sa sarili naming katuwiran,
kundi sa Iyong sagana at dakilang mga awa.
Hindi kami karapat-dapat kahit man lamang pumulot
ng mga mumo sa ilalim ng Iyong hapag.
Gayunman, Ikaw ay Siya ring Panginoon,
na ang likás na katangian ay laging magpakita ng awa.

Ipagkaloob Mo nga sa amin, mapagpalang Panginoon,
na sa pamamagitan ng pananampalataya
aming makain ang laman
ng Iyong minamahal na Anak na si Jesu-Cristo,
at mainom ang Kaniyang dugo,
upang ang aming mga katawang makasalanan ay luminis
sa pamamagitan ng Kaniyang katawan,
at ang aming mga kaluluwa ay mahugasan
sa pamamagitan ng Kaniyang pinakamahalagang dugo,
at upang kami’y manahan sa Kaniya magpakailanman,
at Siya’y manahan sa amin.

Palaguin mo sa amin ang tunay
at buháy na pananampalataya
na sa pamamagitan nito kami’y yumayakap kay Cristo.
Bigyan mo kami ng kakayahang maniwala sa lahat
ng mga pangako ng Ebanghelyo,
at tanggapin sa Banal na Hapunang ito
ang kumpirmasyon ng mga iyon ding pangako.

Gisingin Mo, sa pamamagitan ng Iyong Banal na Espiritu,
ang aming mga kaluluwa mula sa pagkakahimbing,
upang matanggap namin ang Iyong kaligtasan,
nang may pananabik at pasasalamat.
Turuan Mo kaming hintayin
ang aming Panginoong Jesu-Cristo mula sa langit,
na Siyang magdadala sa amin sa Kaniyang sarili
sa walang hanggang kaluwalhatian.

Ito’y aming hinihiling nang may kababaang-loob sa Iyo,
sa pamamagitan ng Iyong Anak na si Jesu-Cristo,
na nabubuhay at naghahari kasama Mo
at ng Banal na Espiritu,
iisa na Diyos, magpakailanman.
Amen.

We do not presume to come to this Your table,
O merciful Lord,
trusting in our own righteousness,
but in Your manifold and great mercies.
We are not worthy so much as to gather up
the crumbs under Your table.
Yet You are the same Lord,
whose very nature is always to show mercy.

Grant us therefore, gracious Lord,
so by faith
to eat the flesh
of Your dear Son, Jesus Christ,
and to drink His blood,
that our sinful bodies
may be made clean by His body,
and our souls washed
through His most precious blood,
and that we may evermore dwell in Him,
and He in us.

Increase in us that true
and living faith
by which we lay hold of Christ.
Enable us to believe all
the promises of the Gospel,
and to receive in this Holy Supper
the confirmation of those very same promises.
 
By Your Holy Spirit,
awaken our souls from slumber,
that we may receive Your salvation,
with eagerness and thanksgiving.
Teach us to await
our Lord Jesus Christ from heaven,
who will bring us to Himself
in everlasting glory.

We humbly ask You this,
through Your Son, Jesus Christ,
who lives and reigns with You and the Holy Spirit,
one God, forever and ever.
Amen.

scripture_selections.text: Oh magsiparito kayo, tayo’y magsiawit sa Panginoon: tayo’y magkaingay na may kagalakan sa malaking bato na ating kaligtasan. Tayo’y magsiharap sa kaniyang harapan na may pagpapasalamat, tayo’y magkaingay na may kagalakan sa kaniya na may mga pagaawitan. Sapagka’t ang Panginoon ay dakilang Dios, at dakilang Hari sa lahat ng mga dios,

O halikayo, tayo’y umawit sa Panginoon;
tayo’y sumigaw na may kagalakan
sa malaking bato ng ating kaligtasan!
Lumapit tayo sa Kanyang harapan
na may pagpapasalamat;
tayo’y sumigaw na may kagalakan
sa Kanya ng mga awit ng pagpupuri!
Sapagkat ang Panginoon
ay dakilang Diyos,
at dakilang Hari sa lahat ng mga diyos.
Nasa Kanyang kamay
ang mga kalaliman ng lupa,
ang mga kataasan ng mga bundok
ay Kanya rin.
Ang dagat ay Kanya,
sapagkat ito’y Kanyang ginawa,
ang Kanyang mga kamay
ang lumikha ng tuyong lupa.
Tapat ang pasabi, at nararapat tanggapin ng lahat, na si Cristo Jesus ay naparito sa sanglibutan upang iligtas ang mga makasalanan.

Na siya rin ang nagdala ng ating mga kasalanan sa kaniyang katawan sa ibabaw ng kahoy, upang pagkamatay natin sa mga kasalanan, ay mangabuhay tayo sa katuwiran; na dahil sa kaniyang mga sugat ay nangagsigaling kayo.
Ikiling mo ang iyong pakinig, Oh Panginoon, at sagutin mo ako; sapagka’t ako’y dukha at mapagkailangan. Oh bumalik ka sa akin, at maawa ka sa akin; ibigay mo ang lakas mo sa iyong lingkod.

At ito ang ibig kong sabihin: Ang naghahasik nang bahagya ay mag-aani rin nang bahagya, at ang naghahasik nang sagana ay mag-aani rin nang sagana. Ang bawat isa ay magbigay ayon sa ipinasiya ng kanyang puso, hindi mabigat sa kalooban, o dala ng pangangailangan, sapagkat iniibig ng Diyos ang nagbibigay na masaya.

At sinalita ng Dios ang lahat ng salitang ito, na sinasabi, Ako ang Panginoon mong Dios, na naglabas sa iyo sa lupain ng Egipto, sa bahay ng pagkaalipin:
**1   Huwag kang magkakaroon ng ibang mga dios sa harap ko.
2   Huwag kang gagawa para sa iyo ng anomang diyus-diyosan.
3   Huwag mong babanggitin ang pangalan ng Panginoon mong Dios sa walang kabuluhan;
4   Alalahanin mo ang araw ng sabbath upang ipangilin. 
5   Igalang mo ang iyong ama at ang iyong ina.
6   Huwag kang papatay.
7   Huwag kang mangangalunya.
8   Huwag kang magnanakaw.
9   Huwag kang magsinungaling laban sa iyong kapuwa.
10   Huwag mong iimbutin ang anomang bagay ng iyong kapuwa.**
Kung ikaw, Panginoon, magtatanda ng mga kasamaan, Oh Panginoon, sinong tatayo? Nguni’t may kapatawarang taglay ka, upang ikaw ay katakutan.

Pakinggan Mo, O Panginoon, ang aking dalangin,
Iyong dinggin ang aking mga daing!
Sa Iyong katapatan, sa Iyong katuwiran,
ako’y Iyong sagutin!
At huwag Kang pumasok na kasama
ng Iyong lingkod sa kahatulan;
sapagkat walang taong nabubuhay
na matuwid sa Iyong harapan.

Ngayon sa Kanya na may kakayahang mag-ingat sa inyo mula sa pagkatisod, at sa inyo’y makapaghaharap na walang kapintasan sa harapan ng Kanyang kaluwalhatian na may malaking kagalakan, sa iisang Diyos na ating Tagapagligtas, sa pamamagitan ni Jesu-Cristo na ating Panginoon, Sumakanya nawa ang kaluwalhatian, ang karangalan, ang kapangyarihan at ang kapamahalaan, bago pa ang lahat ng panahon, at ngayon at magpakailanman. Amen.

Ngayon, sa Kanya na makakagawa ng higit na sagana kaysa lahat ng ating hinihingi o iniisip, ayon sa kapangyarihang gumagawa sa atin, Sumakanya nawa ang kaluwalhatian sa iglesya at kay Cristo Jesus sa lahat ng mga salinlahi, magpakailanpaman. Amen.

Halina kayo, tayo’y magsiawit sa PANGINOON: tayo’y magkaingay na may kagalakan sa malaking bato ng ating kaligtasan.
Halina kayo, tayo’y magsiawit ng mga pasalamat sa kanya, tayo’y magsihiyaw ng may kagalakan sa kanya ng mga pagawitan.
Dinggin ninyo ngayon kung ano ang sinasabi ng Panginoon: Bumangon ka, makipagkaalit ka sa harap ng mga bundok, at dinggin ng mga burol ang iyong tinig.

Each one should give what he has decided in his heart to give, not out of regret or compulsion. For God loves a cheerful giver. And God is able to make all grace abound to you, so that in all things, at all times, having all that you need, you will abound in every good work.

Therefore, as the elect of God, holy and beloved, clothe yourselves with hearts of compassion, kindness, humility, gentleness, and patience. Bear with one another and forgive any complaint you may have against someone else. Forgive as the Lord forgave you. And over all these virtues put on love, which is the bond of perfect unity.

Remember this: Whoever sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and whoever sows generously will also reap generously. Each one should give what he has decided in his heart to give, not out of regret or compulsion. For God loves a cheerful giver.
If You, O LORD, kept track of iniquities, then who, O Lord, could stand? But with You there is forgiveness, so that You may be feared.

Now to Him who is able to keep you from stumbling and to present you unblemished in His glorious presence, with great joy— to the only God our Savior be glory, majesty, dominion, and authority through Jesus Christ our Lord before all time, and now, and for all eternity. Amen.

Sapagka't gayon na lamang ang pagsinta ng Dios sa sanglibutan, na ibinigay niya ang kaniyang bugtong na Anak, upang ang sinomang sa kaniya'y sumampalataya ay huwag mapahamak, kundi magkaroon ng buhay na walang hanggan.

Therefore let it be known to you, brothers, that through Jesus the forgiveness of sins is proclaimed to you. Through Him everyone who believes is justified from everything from which you could not be justified by the law of Moses.

Kaya maging hayag nawa sa inyo, mga kapatid, na sa pamamagitan ng taong ito'y ibinabalita sa inyo ang kapatawaran ng mga kasalanan: At sa pamamagitan niya ang bawa't nananampalataya ay inaaring ganap sa lahat ng mga bagay, na sa mga ito'y hindi kayo aariing ganap sa pamamagitan ng kautusan ni Moises.

Now He who supplies seed to the sower and bread for food will supply and multiply your store of seed and will increase the harvest of your righteousness. You will be enriched in every way to be generous on every occasion, and through us your generosity will produce thanksgiving to God. For this ministry of service is not only supplying the needs of the saints but is also overflowing in many expressions of thanksgiving to God.

God has ascended amid shouts of joy, the LORD with the sound of the horn. Sing praises to God, sing praises; sing praises to our King, sing praises! For God is King of all the earth; sing to Him a psalm of praise. God reigns over the nations; God is seated on His holy throne. The nobles of the nations have assembled as the people of the God of Abraham; for the shields of the earth belong to God; He is highly exalted.

O Lord, hear my prayer.
In Your faithfulness, give ear to my plea;
in Your righteousness, answer me.
Do not bring Your servant into judgment,
for no one alive is righteous before You.

Now to Him who is able to do immeasurably more than all we ask or imagine, according to His power that is at work within us, to Him be the glory in the church and in Christ Jesus throughout all generations, forever and ever. Amen.

Siyang nagbibigay ng binhi sa manghahasik at ng tinapay bilang pagkain ay Siyang magbibigay at magpaparami ng inyong binhi upang ihasik, at magpaparami ng mga bunga ng inyong pagiging matuwid; na kayo ay pinayayaman sa bawat bagay dahil sa inyong kagandahang-loob, na sa pamamagitan namin ay nagbubunga ng pasasalamat sa Diyos. Sapagkat ang pangangasiwa sa paglilingkod na ito ay hindi lamang pumupuno sa pangangailangan ng mga banal, kundi umaapaw rin sa pamamagitan ng maraming pasasalamat sa Diyos.

Come, let us sing for joy to the LORD; let us shout to the Rock of our salvation! Let us enter His presence with thanksgiving; let us make a joyful noise to Him in song. For the LORD is a great God, a great King above all gods.

Come, let us sing for joy to the LORD; let us shout to the Rock of our salvation! Let us enter His presence with thanksgiving; let us make a joyful noise to Him in song. For the LORD is a great God, a great King above all gods. In His hand are the depths of the earth, and the mountain peaks belong to Him. The sea is His, for He made it, and His hands formed the dry land.
Come, let us sing for joy to the LORD; let us shout to the Rock of our salvation!
Hear now what the LORD says: “Arise, plead your case before the mountains, and let the hills hear your voice.

And God spoke all these words: I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery.
**1    You shall have no other gods before Me.
2    You shall not make for yourself an idol.
3    You shall not take the name of the LORD your God in vain.
4    Remember the Sabbath day by keeping it holy.
5    Honor your father and mother.
6    You shall not murder.
7    You shall not commit adultery.
8    You shall not steal.
9    You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor.
10   You shall not covet anything that belongs to your neighbor.**

Mangagbihis nga kayo gaya ng mga hinirang ng Dios, na mga banal at minamahal, ng isang pusong mahabagin, ng kagandahangloob, ng kababaan, ng kaamuan, ng pagpapahinuhod: Mangagtiisan kayo sa isa’t isa, at mangagpatawaran kayo sa isa’t isa, kung ang sinoman ay may sumbong laban sa kanino man; na kung paanong pinatawad kayo ng Panginoon, ay gayon din naman ang inyong gawin: At sa ibabaw ng lahat ng mga bagay na ito ay mangagbihis kayo ng pagibig na siyang tali ng kasakdalan.

Magbigay ang bawa’t isa ayon sa ipinasiya ng kaniyang puso: huwag mabigat sa loob, o dahil sa kailangan: sapagka’t iniibig ng Dios ang nagbibigay na masaya. At maaaring gawin ng Dios na ang lahat ng biyaya ay magsisagana sa inyo; upang kayo, na mayroong laging buong kaya sa lahat ay magsipanagana sa bawa’t mabuting gawa:
This is a trustworthy saying, worthy of full acceptance: Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners, of whom I am the worst.
He Himself bore our sins in His body on the tree, so that we might die to sin and live to righteousness. “By His stripes you are healed.”
A prayer of David. Incline Your ear, O LORD, and answer me, for I am poor and needy. Turn to me and have mercy; grant Your strength to Your servant; save the son of Your maidservant.

Each one should give what he has decided in his heart to give, not out of regret or compulsion. For God loves a cheerful giver. And God is able to make all grace abound to you, so that in all things, at all times, having all that you need, you will abound in every good work.

Then came the day of Unleavened Bread on which the Passover lamb was to be sacrificed. Jesus sent Peter and John, saying, “Go and make preparations for us to eat the Passover.” “Where do You want us to prepare it?” they asked. He answered, “When you enter the city, a man carrying a jug of water will meet you. Follow him to the house he enters, and say to the owner of that house, ‘The Teacher asks: Where is the guest room, where I may eat the Passover with My disciples?’ And he will show you a large upper room, already furnished. Make preparations there.” So they went and found it just as Jesus had told them. And they prepared the Passover. When the hour had come, Jesus reclined at the table with His apostles. And He said to them, “I have eagerly desired to eat this Passover with you before My suffering. For I tell you that I will not eat it again until it is fulfilled in the kingdom of God.” After taking the cup, He gave thanks and said, “Take this and divide it among yourselves. For I tell you that I will not drink of the fruit of the vine from now on until the kingdom of God comes.” And He took the bread, gave thanks and broke it, and gave it to them, saying, “This is My body, given for you; do this in remembrance of Me.” In the same way, after supper He took the cup, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in My blood, which is poured out for you. Look! The hand of My betrayer is with Mine on the table.

Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called sons of God. Blessed are those who are persecuted because of righteousness, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven. Blessed are you when people insult you, persecute you, and falsely say all kinds of evil against you because of Me. Rejoice and be glad, because great is your reward in heaven, for in the same way they persecuted the prophets before you. You are the salt of the earth. But if the salt loses its savor, how can it be made salty again? It is no longer good for anything, except to be thrown out and trampled by men. You are the light of the world. A city on a hill cannot be hidden. Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a basket. Instead, they set it on a stand, and it gives light to everyone in the house.

Come, let us sing for joy to the LORD; let us shout to the Rock of our salvation! Let us enter His presence with thanksgiving; let us make a joyful noise to Him in song. For the LORD is a great God, a great King above all gods. In His hand are the depths of the earth, and the mountain peaks belong to Him. The sea is His, for He made it, and His hands formed the dry land. O come, let us worship and bow down; let us kneel before the LORD our Maker.

Oh magsiparito kayo, tayo'y magsiawit sa Panginoon: tayo'y magkaingay na may kagalakan sa malaking bato na ating kaligtasan. Tayo'y magsiharap sa kaniyang harapan na may pagpapasalamat, tayo'y magkaingay na may kagalakan sa kaniya na may mga pagaawitan. Sapagka't ang Panginoon ay dakilang Dios, at dakilang Hari sa lahat ng mga dios, Na sa kaniyang kamay ang mga malalim na dako ng lupa, ang mga kataasan ng mga bundok ay kaniya rin. Ang dagat ay kaniya, at kaniyang ginawa: at ang kaniyang mga kamay ay lumikha ng tuyong lupa. Oh magsiparito kayo, tayo'y magsisamba at magsiyukod; tayo'y magsiluhod sa harap ng Panginoon na May-lalang sa atin.
If we say we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us. If we confess our sins, He is faithful and just to forgive us our sins and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.

Kung sinasabi nating tayo'y walang kasalanan, ay ating dinadaya ang ating sarili, at ang katotohanan ay wala sa atin. Kung ipinahahayag natin ang ating mga kasalanan, ay tapat at banal siya na tayo'y patatawarin sa ating mga kasalanan, at tayo'y lilinisin sa lahat ng kalikuan.
For God so loved the world that He gave His one and only Son, that everyone who believes in Him shall not perish but have eternal life.

O Diyos, dalangin ko’y pakinggan Mo,
pakinggan Mo ang mga salita ng bibig ko.
Ang Diyos ang sa aki’y tumutulong;
ang PANGINOON ang umaalalay sa aking kaluluwa.
Ako’y mag-aalay sa Iyo ng kusang-loob na handog;
ako’y magpapasalamat sa Iyong pangalan,
O PANGINOON, sapagkat ito’y mabuti.
